name: milestone-oracle-diagnostic
description: A 354-family math-reasoning diagnostic for milestone-oracle probing. Each family pairs a parent math problem with a teacher-compiled set of typed, symbolically checkable milestones (intermediate sub-goals with gold answers). The dataset supports a judge-free diagnostic protocol that probes a student model under three help levels (no help / milestone roadmap / roadmap + gold milestone answers) and a Stage 0 milestone-test pass/fail check, yielding a five-state reasoning-gap taxonomy (roadmap, milestone-execution, composition, missing-milestone, capability). Source problems are derived from NuminaMath-1.5-RL-Verifiable; milestones are produced by GPT-5.4 with thinking mode enabled and validated by deterministic symbolic verification. Released alongside the paper 'Diagnosing Math-Reasoning Failure Structure with Milestone Oracles'.
citeAs: Anonymous. Diagnosing Math-Reasoning Failure Structure with Milestone Oracles. NeurIPS 2026 Datasets and Benchmarks Track (under review).
rai:dataCollection: Source problems are sampled from NuminaMath-1.5-RL-Verifiable (a public competition-math dataset). For each parent problem, a strong teacher model (GPT-5.4 with thinking mode enabled) is prompted to produce a typed milestone decomposition, with each milestone given a self-contained description, an answer schema (one of NUMBER, EXPR, EQUATION, INEQUALITY, SET, INTERVAL, TUPLE_LIST, MATRIX_VECTOR, MCQ_STRING), and a canonical answer. Schema enforcement, anti-leakage filtering, standalone-solvability, and family-level validity are applied during compilation (see paper Algorithm 1).
rai:dataCollectionMissingData: Of 2000 candidate parents, 1024 received valid milestone decompositions; 731 also passed the Qwen-base anchor screen; 354 had at least two non-INTEGRATE milestones and form the main diagnostic. The 32-family held-out slice is a separate compilation from a disjoint 150-parent NuminaMath sample. The 731-family compiled set is included for transparency.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Symbolic answer normalization via the verifier cascade (boxed extraction, schema-aware parse, sympy equivalence, math-verify). Families with milestone canonical answers symbolically equivalent to the parent answer are flagged for leak-safe handling.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Milestone types and answer schemas are assigned by the teacher model and validated by deterministic checks. Audit annotations classify 100 unrecovered families using a fixed 5-category rubric (genuine composition / decomposition incomplete / verifier artifact / not standalone / beyond capability) with a documented disagreement-resolution rule. Inter-rater kappa is 0.49 on the 100-family set, 0.64 on a 50-family validation subset.
rai:dataUseCases: Diagnostic evaluation of math-reasoning models that reports failure-mode structure rather than only aggregate accuracy. Cross-model failure-fingerprint comparison on a fixed problem set. Longitudinal tracking of how training (RL, SFT, etc.) moves problem families through diagnostic states.
rai:dataLimitations: Diagnostic is anchored on Qwen3-8B-Base screening; it is a fixed slice rather than a model-neutral sample. Milestones are compiled by a single teacher (GPT-5.4); a small Claude-Sonnet pilot reproduces the qualitative pattern but is underpowered. Symbolic verification scope means problems requiring proof writing or non-symbolic outputs are out of scope. Source problems come from public benchmarks; standard public-benchmark contamination caveats apply.
rai:dataBiases: Three known sources of bias. (1) Anchor-model bias: the 354-family diagnostic set is the subset of NuminaMath candidates for which Qwen3-8B-Base fails the parent under direct prompting but solves every Stage 0 milestone in isolation. The set is therefore over-representative of problems where Qwen-base shows a parent/milestone gap, and under-representative of problems that are either trivial or beyond Qwen-base milestone-level ability. (2) Teacher-decomposition bias: milestone roadmaps are compiled by a single teacher (GPT-5.4 with thinking mode); a different teacher would yield different roadmaps and could change which reasoning gaps are exposed. A second-teacher pilot using Claude Sonnet 4.6 reproduces the qualitative specificity pattern but is underpowered (n=8). (3) Source-distribution bias: NuminaMath-1.5-RL-Verifiable is biased toward competition-style symbolic-answer problems, so the diagnostic does not exercise proof writing, geometric construction, or other non-symbolic outputs. None of these biases prevent the cross-model and longitudinal contrasts we report, but they constrain the population to which absolute counts generalize.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The dataset contains no personal, private, or sensitive information. Source problems are mathematics word problems and competition problems from a publicly released math benchmark (NuminaMath-1.5-RL-Verifiable). Milestone roadmaps and gold answers are LLM-generated mathematical content. Audit annotations are technical category labels (genuine composition / decomposition incomplete / verifier artifact / not standalone / beyond capability) produced by the authors; no human-subject data is included.

distribution.name: diagnostic_354_families.jsonl
distribution.description: Main diagnostic set: 354 milestone families with parent problems, gold answers, typed milestones, and pre-RL Qwen-base milestone solve rates.

distribution.name: diagnostic_731_compiled.jsonl
distribution.description: Pre-restriction 731-family compiled set kept for provenance.

distribution.name: diagnostic_held_out_32.jsonl
distribution.description: 32-family held-out slice compiled from a disjoint 150-parent NuminaMath sample.

distribution.name: oracle_panel_16k
distribution.description: Per-(model, condition, family) parent-probe correctness counts for the six-model panel at max_tokens=16384 with K=8 rollouts per cell. One file per model.

distribution.name: audit_consensus.jsonl
distribution.description: 100-family unrecovered audit: consensus rubric category per family, plus per-rater labels.

distribution.name: audit_rater_a.jsonl
distribution.description: Per-family rubric labels from rater A.

distribution.name: audit_rater_b.jsonl
distribution.description: Per-family rubric labels from rater B.

distribution.name: oracle_leak_safe_repair.jsonl
distribution.description: Oracle re-evaluation of decomposition-incomplete families after leak-safe repair (45 of 50 attempted; the 5 that fail leak-safe condition (1) are excluded).

distribution.name: repaired_decomp_leak_safe.jsonl
distribution.description: Regenerated milestone families after leak-safe decomposition repair.

distribution.name: repaired_verifier.jsonl
distribution.description: Verifier-repaired families (re-solved gold answer + canonical replacement) for the 26 verifier-artifact rubric category.

recordSet.name: milestone_family
recordSet.description: One milestone family per record. The parent is a math problem; each milestone is a self-contained typed sub-goal with its own description, answer schema, and gold answer.

field.name: parent_prompt
field.description: Verbatim student-facing parent problem prompt (with system-side boxed-answer instruction).

field.name: parent_answer
field.description: Canonical gold answer for the parent problem (LaTeX string).

field.name: parent_note
field.description: Verifier acceptance note for the parent answer.

recordSet.name: oracle_panel_record
recordSet.description: One row per (model, condition, family). Records the K=8 rollout count of correct parent answers under each oracle condition at max_tokens=16384.

field.name: condition
field.description: Probing condition: C1_direct, C2_descriptions, C3_gold_answers, C2_random, C2_generic, or C3_mismatched.

field.name: n_correct
field.description: Number of correct rollouts out of K=8.

field.name: k
field.description: Total rollouts attempted (always 8 in this release).
